last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

navigation.confidence_reason: The current view is already a hoare/phoare procedure state, so the next route should advance the current frontier rather than restart with byphoare.
navigation.why_now: current goal is a one-sided procedure judgment; use state-local procedure tactics for this frontier.

preconditions: Use when the view is already a hoare/phoare procedure judgment.
Do not prefix this with byphoare; the proof is already inside the one-sided judgment.
Probe the bounded block first; commit only accepted block or prefix.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: A one-sided procedure body/frontier, loop obligation, or pure residual subgoal.

anti_routes.reason: The proof is already inside a hoare/phoare judgment.
One-sided hoare/phoare states do not have left/right pRHL branches.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect tactic_forms for hoare/phoare procedure forms
inspect goal_info for the current one-sided procedure frontier
inspect subgoal_gap or inv_from_lemma for loop invariant shape
downgrade this route and choose another current-view navigation item

moves.title: Proof-route context
moves.guidance: Structured procedure frontier: straight_line_prefix_before_call_site; primary: use sp if the next invariant/seq cut needs names from the straight-line prefix
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

moves.title: Proof-route context
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
moves.why_relevant: A straight-line symbolic prefix is visible before the next control boundary; sp names that prefix so if/while/rnd can be handled with explicit variables.

moves.title: Proof-route context
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
moves.why_relevant: Procedure body statements are exposed; wp is the backward pass that consumes assign/call/sample suffixes and reveals the next control-flow obligation.

moves.title: Proof-route context
moves.guidance: Inspect proof obligation stack: direct_procedure_call / G4.A.guess / statement 4.
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: setup statements appear before the current frontier; call sites are visible at the frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

first_instruction_alignment.right_first: no matching right-side call at this frontier
first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: The visible frontier is one-sided; use a one-sided tactic, alignment step, or seq cut before applying a symmetric tactic.

rows.left: 3 setup statement(s): G2.v <- H G1.k (G3.a, G3.a_, G3.c); G2.alpha <- (r - G1.u * (G1.x + G2.v * G1.y)) / (G1.w * (G1.u' - G1.u)); G1.cstar <- Some (G3.a, G3.a_, G3.c, G3.d)
rows.right: no right-side setup before this frontier
rows.proof_read: The left side has setup before the shared frontier; relate it to the precondition or carry the initialized state in the next invariant/cut.

rows.right: no matching right-side call at this frontier
rows.proof_read: This is a call frontier; compare the procedures/oracle wrappers before choosing a named call or invariant call.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

absorb_depth.sp_hint: sp 3 0 / wp -3 -0 absorbs the straight-line setup (3 left, 0 right) before the next frontier

checks: Check the next statement/call frontier before applying local transforms.
Keep one-sided instrumentation as a map, not as a tactic recipe.

cut_position.tactic: seq 1 : (g ^ G1.u \in map (fun (t : PKE_.ciphertext) => t.`1) G3.cilog /\ G1.g_ ^ G1.u' \in map (fun (t : PKE_.ciphertext) => t.`2) G3.cilog /\ g ^ r' \in map (fun (t : PKE_.ciphertext) => t.`3) G3.cilog /\ size G3.cilog <= PKE_.qD /\ G1.w <> zero /\ G1.g_ = g ^ G1.w) (PKE_.qD%r / order%r) (PKE_.qD%r / order%r) 1%r 0%r.

selected_handles.title: Proof-route context
selected_handles.why_relevant: Structured procedure frontier: straight_line_prefix_before_call_site; primary: use sp if the next invariant/seq cut needs names from the straight-line prefix

selected_handles.title: Proof-route context
selected_handles.why_relevant: A straight-line symbolic prefix is visible before the next control boundary; sp names that prefix so if/while/rnd can be handled with explicit variables.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
The current procedure frontier exposes a call route.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid form for call, while, seq, rnd, or rewrite.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid one-sided hoare/phoare loop form.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery

items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #82 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #80 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Return to resume start
items.committed_tactic: conseq (: _ ==> (g ^ G1.u \in map (fun (t : ciphertext) => t.`1) G3.cilog) /\ (G1.g_ ^ G1.u' \in map (fun (t : ciphertext) => t.`2) G3.cilog) /\ (g ^ r' \in map (fun (t : ciphertext) => t.`3) G3.cilog) /\ (g ^ r \in map (fun (t : ciphertext) => t.`4) G3.cilog) /\ size G3.cilog <= PKE_.qD /\ G1.w <> zero /\ G1.g_ = g ^ G1.w).
items.why_checkpoint: resume boundary; selecting it preserves the verified replay prefix and removes tactics committed after resume
